title: Gooey Moroccan Dates Tagine
description: A sweet and savory Moroccan dish featuring pitted dates, perfect for a lazy Sunday meal. This recipe is inspired by traditional Ayurvedic and Sattvic cooking methods.

ingredients.name: Dates, pitted
ingredients.notes: fresh or dried dates work well in this recipe

instructions: Heat the coconut oil in a large clay or ceramic tagine over medium heat.
Add the onion and ginger and cook until the onion is translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the cumin, cinnamon, paprika, and salt, and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the pitted dates, chopped parsley, lemon juice, and water to the tagine, and stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a simmer, then reduce the heat to low and cook, covered, for 20 minutes, or until the dates are tender and the sauce is thick and syrupy.
If using dried apricots, add them to the tagine and cook for an additional 5 minutes, or until they are rehydrated and tender.
Serve the tagine hot, garnished with additional parsley if desired.
Refrigerate or freeze leftovers for up to 1 week.
Reheat the tagine over low heat, adding a little water if necessary to thin the sauce.
